message:
feat(core,abi): port Ethernet address family to core::ether (bd-eth-epic)

Lifts the Ethernet address parser, formatter, and /etc/ethers line
parser (~135 lines combined: parse_hex_nibble + parse_ether_addr +
format_ether_addr + ether_line) out of unistd_abi.rs into a pure-safe
frankenlibc_core::ether module.

New core surface:
  pub type EtherAddr = [u8; 6];
  pub const ETHER_ADDR_TEXT_LEN: usize = 17;
  pub fn parse_ether_addr(bytes: &[u8]) -> Option<EtherAddr>
  pub fn format_ether_addr(addr: &EtherAddr) -> [u8; 17]
  pub fn parse_ether_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<(EtherAddr, &[u8])>

The parser:
  - accepts colon-separated 1-or-2 hex digits per octet (matching
    glibc ether_aton, which folds `0:0:0:0:0:0` into all-zero)
  - case-insensitive
  - rejects trailing garbage including a 7th `:xx` octet

format_ether_addr returns the 17-byte canonical lowercase form
(`xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx`) without a NUL terminator, so callers can
splice it into either NUL-terminated or fixed-length buffers.

parse_ether_line trims leading whitespace, extracts the MAC up to
the next whitespace, then returns a borrowed hostname slice
terminated by whitespace, EOL, or NUL — matching the contract that
ether_ntohost relies on.

abi changes:
  - parse_ether_addr (FFI shim): CStr -> bytes -> core; writes octet
    array into caller's EtherAddrBytes on success.
  - format_ether_addr (FFI shim): copies the 17-byte core output
    plus a NUL into the 18-byte caller buffer.
  - ether_line: now ~12 lines — reads CStr, calls
    core::ether::parse_ether_line, copies hostname slice +
    NUL-terminator into caller buffer.

EtherAddrBytes (#[repr(C)] wrapper around [u8;6]) stays in abi
as a layout-stable type for the C `struct ether_addr` ABI.

Verified:
  - 23 new core::ether unit tests pass
  - 1/1 ether_line_parses_valid_ethers_entry abi test passes
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass

Closes bd-eth-epic.
